project.name: app-security
project.description: Desktop security application that protects users on public WiFi/LAN networks by providing network protection (VPN, kill switch, evil twin/ARP spoof detection, firewall, DNS encryption) and device anonymity (MAC randomization, hostname spoofing, traffic obfuscation, fingerprint masking, DNS leak prevention). Pre-implementation project with detailed business requirements and architecture planning.

nodes.name: Network Protection
nodes.summary: Provides active defense against network-level threats on public WiFi/LAN. Includes VPN auto-connect, kill switch, firewall hardening, and DNS encryption to prevent MITM attacks, packet sniffing, DNS spoofing, and traffic interception.

businessRules: VPN must activate automatically when connecting to an unknown/untrusted network
Kill switch must block all internet traffic if VPN connection drops
Firewall rules must be applied on connection and reverted on app shutdown
DNS queries must never leak outside the encrypted tunnel
All network changes must save original state for rollback on exit
crossDomainInteractions: Feeds network state to Detection domain for threat analysis
Sends threat alerts to Alert System domain
Reads trust scores from Detection domain to determine VPN activation
Must coordinate with Anonymity domain to avoid conflicts when changing network state

nodes.name: Device Anonymity
nodes.summary: Hides device identity and prevents tracking on public networks. Includes MAC address randomization, hostname spoofing, traffic padding, multi-hop proxy routing, browser fingerprint masking, and DNS leak prevention to make the device unidentifiable to network observers.

businessRules: Original MAC and hostname must be saved before spoofing and restored on app exit
MAC randomization must generate vendor-pliant random addresses
Traffic padding must not exceed 5% network overhead
DNS queries must be routed through the VPN tunnel only
Fingerprint masking must produce a common/generic browser profile
crossDomainInteractions: MAC/hostname changes are part of the network stack, coordinated with Network Protection
DNS leak prevention relies on the VPN tunnel from Network Protection domain
Alert System is notified if anonymization modules fail or encounter errors

nodes.name: Threat Detection
nodes.summary: Passively and actively monitors the local network for attacks in real time. Detects evil twin access points, ARP spoofing attacks, DNS spoofing attempts, and computes a network trust score to guide automatic protection decisions.

businessRules: Evil twin detection must compare beacon SSID/BSSID against known networks
ARP spoof detection must flag duplicate IP-MAC mappings or gateway MAC changes
Trust score must aggregate multiple signals (encryption type, known AP, packet anomalies)
Detection must require libpcap/npcap and root/admin privileges
False positives must be handled via configurable thresholds and user whitelists
crossDomainInteractions: Triggers automatic VPN activation in Network Protection when trust score is low
Sends real-time alerts to Alert System when threats are detected
Detection results feed into the trust scoring algorithm

nodes.name: Alert System
nodes.summary: Provides real-time notifications and logging when threats are detected or protection modules encounter issues. Delivers alerts via system tray notifications, logs events, and maintains a history of detected incidents.

businessRules: Alerts must be delivered in real time with no perceptible delay
Critical alerts (MITM active, VPN down with kill switch) must be persistent until acknowledged
All events must be logged with timestamps for incident review
Alert severity levels must be configurable by the user
crossDomainInteractions: Receives threat events from Detection domain (evil twin, ARP spoof)
Receives status events from Network Protection (VPN connected/disconnected, kill switch triggered)
Receives failure events from Anonymity domain (MAC randomization failed)

nodes.name: Core Engine
nodes.summary: Manages the application lifecycle, module orchestration, configuration, and system tray UI. Coordinates startup/shutdown of all security modules, handles OS privilege escalation, and provides the user interface for monitoring and control.

businessRules: App must request and verify admin/root privileges at startup
Each module must be independently activatable/deactivatable without affecting others
Configuration changes must persist across app restarts
On app crash or forced exit, the kill switch must have a failsafe to restore normal network
The UI must display current protection status, active threats, and trust score
crossDomainInteractions: Starts and stops all modules in Network Protection, Anonymity, Detection, and Alert domains
Propagates configuration changes to all modules
Collects status from all domains for UI display

nodes.name: VPN Auto-Connect
nodes.summary: Automatically detects when the device joins an untrusted network and establishes a WireGuard VPN tunnel to encrypt all traffic before any data is transmitted.

nodes.name: Kill Switch
nodes.summary: Monitors VPN connection health and immediately blocks all outbound internet traffic if the VPN tunnel drops, preventing any unencrypted data from leaking.

nodes.name: Firewall Hardening
nodes.summary: Scans current firewall rules, saves the original state, applies hardened rules to block unnecessary incoming ports and prevent port scanning, and reverts on app exit.

nodes.name: DNS Encryption
nodes.summary: Intercepts all DNS queries from the system and routes them through DNS-over-HTTPS or DNS-over-TLS to prevent DNS spoofing and eavesdropping.

nodes.name: MAC Address Randomization
nodes.summary: Reads the current MAC address, saves it, generates a vendor-compliant random MAC address, applies it to the network interface, and restores the original on app exit.

nodes.name: Hostname Spoofing
nodes.summary: Reads the current hostname, saves it, generates a generic random hostname, applies it to hide the device from DHCP and mDNS discovery, and restores on exit.

nodes.name: Browser Fingerprint Masking
nodes.summary: Modifies TLS ClientHello messages and HTTP headers using uTLS to present a generic browser fingerprint, preventing device identification via TLS or HTTP header analysis.

nodes.name: DNS Leak Prevention
nodes.summary: Enforces that all DNS queries only travel through the VPN tunnel, blocks the system DNS resolver, monitors DNS packets for leaks, and alerts if any DNS query escapes the tunnel.

nodes.name: Evil Twin Detection
nodes.summary: Captures WiFi beacon frames, compares SSID/BSSID against known networks, checks for signal strength anomalies, classifies threat level, and triggers alerts for suspected evil twin access points.

nodes.name: ARP Spoof Detection
nodes.summary: Captures ARP packets, builds and monitors the ARP table for duplicate IP-MAC mappings and gateway MAC changes, and alerts when ARP spoofing is suspected.

nodes.name: Network Trust Scoring
nodes.summary: Collects network metadata (encryption type, known AP history, packet anomalies), computes a composite trust score, and recommends a protection level to guide automatic VPN and firewall activation.

nodes.name: Real-Time Alerting
nodes.summary: Receives threat events from detection and protection modules, classifies severity, displays system tray notifications, and writes all events to an persistent event log.

nodes.name: Application Lifecycle
nodes.summary: Manages the full application lifecycle from requesting admin privileges, loading configuration, initializing all security modules, running the system tray UI, through graceful shutdown with full network state revert.

nodes.name: Detect Network Change
nodes.summary: Monitors OS network interfaces for connection events (WiFi join, Ethernet plug-in) to trigger the VPN auto-connect flow.

nodes.name: Evaluate Network Trust
nodes.summary: Consults the trust scoring module to determine whether the newly joined network is trusted or requires VPN activation.

nodes.name: Establish WireGuard Tunnel
nodes.summary: Initiates a WireGuard VPN connection using the configured server credentials and cryptographic keys.

nodes.name: Verify Tunnel Active
nodes.summary: Confirms the VPN tunnel is established by checking interface status and performing a connectivity test through the tunnel.

nodes.name: Redirect Traffic Through VPN
nodes.summary: Updates system routing table to direct all outbound traffic through the VPN tunnel interface.

nodes.name: Monitor VPN Connection
nodes.summary: Continuously polls the VPN tunnel interface status and sends keepalive probes to detect connection health.

nodes.name: Detect VPN Drop
nodes.summary: Identifies when the VPN tunnel goes down due to server failure, network change, or timeout.

nodes.name: Block All Outbound Traffic
nodes.summary: Immediately applies firewall rules to block all outbound internet traffic except VPN reconnection attempts, preventing unencrypted data leaks.

nodes.name: Notify User and Log
nodes.summary: Sends a critical alert to the user via system tray notification that the kill switch has been activated, and logs the event.

nodes.name: Attempt VPN Reconnect
nodes.summary: Periodically attempts to re-establish the VPN tunnel. If successful, restores normal traffic routing and deactivates the kill switch.

nodes.name: Scan Current Rules
nodes.summary: Reads the existing OS firewall ruleset to understand the current security posture before making changes.

nodes.name: Save Original State
nodes.summary: Persists the current firewall configuration so it can be fully restored when the app shuts down or crashes.

nodes.name: Apply Hardened Rules
nodes.summary: Applies a set of hardened firewall rules that block unnecessary incoming ports and restrict traffic to known-safe destinations.

nodes.name: Block Incoming Ports
nodes.summary: Specifically blocks common attack vectors: open ports that respond to port scanning, SMB, RDP, and other commonly exploited services.

nodes.name: Revert on Exit
nodes.summary: Restores the original firewall ruleset from the saved state when the application shuts down or encounters a fatal error.

nodes.name: Intercept DNS Queries
nodes.summary: Hooks into the OS DNS resolution path to intercept all outgoing DNS queries before they reach the system resolver.

nodes.name: Route Through DoH/DoT
nodes.summary: Sends the intercepted DNS query over an encrypted DNS-over-HTTPS or DNS-over-TLS connection to the configured resolver.

nodes.name: Validate DNS Response
nodes.summary: Validates the DNS response for integrity and checks for signs of DNS spoofing or tampering.

nodes.name: Cache and Return
nodes.summary: Caches the validated DNS response locally and returns it to the requesting application.

nodes.name: Read Current MAC
nodes.summary: Queries the OS network interface to read the current hardware MAC address of the active network adapter.

nodes.name: Save Original MAC
nodes.summary: Persists the original MAC address to ensure it can be restored when the app exits or crashes.

nodes.name: Generate Random MAC
nodes.summary: Generates a new random MAC address with a locally-administered bit set, using a random vendor OUI prefix to appear as a common device.

nodes.name: Apply New MAC
nodes.summary: Disables the network interface, applies the new MAC address via OS-specific syscalls, and re-enables the interface.

nodes.name: Verify Change
nodes.summary: Reads back the MAC address from the interface to confirm the randomization was successfully applied.

nodes.name: Restore on Exit
nodes.summary: Restores the original MAC address from saved state when the application shuts down.

nodes.name: Read Current Hostname
nodes.summary: Reads the current system hostname that would be exposed via DHCP requests and mDNS announcements.

nodes.name: Save Original Hostname
nodes.summary: Persists the original hostname for restoration when the application exits.

nodes.name: Generate Random Hostname
nodes.summary: Generates a generic hostname (e.g., 'ubuntu-desktop', 'android-phone') that does not reveal the actual device identity.

nodes.name: Apply Hostname Change
nodes.summary: Sets the new hostname at the OS level so DHCP and mDNS use the spoofed name.

nodes.name: Restore on Exit
nodes.summary: Restores the original hostname from saved state when the application shuts down.

nodes.name: Intercept TLS Client Hello
nodes.summary: Uses uTLS to intercept outgoing TLS handshakes and replace the ClientHello with a generic browser fingerprint profile.

nodes.name: Modify HTTP Headers
nodes.summary: Strips or modifies identifying HTTP headers (User-Agent, Accept-Language, etc.) to present a generic browser profile.

nodes.name: Apply Generic Profile
nodes.summary: Applies a consistent generic browser fingerprint profile that matches common browser configurations, avoiding unique combinations.

nodes.name: Validate Masking
nodes.summary: Tests the applied fingerprint against known fingerprinting services to verify the device is not uniquely identifiable.

nodes.name: Enforce VPN-Only DNS
nodes.summary: Configures the system to only accept DNS queries through the VPN tunnel interface, blocking all other DNS paths.

nodes.name: Block System DNS
nodes.summary: Blocks the OS default DNS resolver (typically port 53 UDP/TCP) from being used directly, forcing all queries through the encrypted tunnel.

nodes.name: Monitor DNS Packets
nodes.summary: Uses packet capture to continuously monitor for any DNS packets attempting to leave the system outside the VPN tunnel.

nodes.name: Alert on Leak Attempt
nodes.summary: Triggers an immediate alert if any DNS query is detected outside the VPN tunnel, indicating a potential DNS leak.

nodes.name: Capture Beacon Frames
nodes.summary: Uses libpcap to capture WiFi beacon frames broadcast by nearby access points, extracting SSID, BSSID, signal strength, and encryption details.

nodes.name: Compare SSID/BSSID
nodes.summary: Compares captured beacon SSID and BSSID against the currently connected network and any known/saved networks to detect duplicates with different BSSIDs.

nodes.name: Check Signal Strength Anomaly
nodes.summary: Analyzes signal strength patterns for anomalies such as a fake AP with unusually strong signal compared to the legitimate AP.

nodes.name: Classify Threat Level
nodes.summary: Combines SSID/BSSID comparison and signal analysis results to classify the evil twin threat as low, medium, or high confidence.

nodes.name: Trigger Alert
nodes.summary: Sends a high-priority alert to the Alert System with details about the suspected evil twin AP.

nodes.name: Capture ARP Packets
nodes.summary: Uses libpcap to capture all ARP request and reply packets on the local network segment.

nodes.name: Build ARP Table
nodes.summary: Maintains a real-time ARP table mapping IP addresses to MAC addresses observed on the local network.

nodes.name: Check Duplicate IP-MAC
nodes.summary: Detects when the same IP address is associated with multiple MAC addresses, which is a strong indicator of ARP spoofing.

nodes.name: Verify Gateway MAC
nodes.summary: Checks that the gateway IP's MAC address remains consistent and matches the expected value, flagging changes as potential spoofing.

nodes.name: Alert and Log
nodes.summary: Sends an alert to the Alert System with ARP spoof details and logs the detection event for incident review.

nodes.name: Collect Network Metadata
nodes.summary: Gathers network metadata including SSID, BSSID, encryption type, signal strength, connected clients count, and network history.

nodes.name: Evaluate Encryption Type
nodes.summary: Assesses the network's encryption (WPA3, WPA2, WEP, open) and assigns a trust contribution based on encryption strength.

nodes.name: Check Known Networks
nodes.summary: Compares the current network against a user-maintained list of known/trusted networks to boost or reduce the trust score.

nodes.name: Analyze Packet Anomalies
nodes.summary: Examines captured packet patterns for anomalies such as unusual ARP traffic, broadcast storms, or abnormal DNS responses that reduce trust.

nodes.name: Compute Trust Score
nodes.summary: Aggregates all trust factors (encryption, known network, packet anomalies) into a composite 0-100 trust score.

nodes.name: Recommend Protection Level
nodes.summary: Translates the trust score into a recommended protection level (none, basic, full) that triggers appropriate VPN and firewall actions.

nodes.name: Receive Threat Event
nodes.summary: Subscribes to the internal event bus and receives threat events from all detection and protection modules.

nodes.name: Classify Severity
nodes.summary: Assigns a severity level (info, warning, critical) to each incoming event based on threat type and confidence.

nodes.name: Show System Tray Notification
nodes.summary: Displays a desktop notification via the system tray with the alert message, severity color, and optional action buttons.

nodes.name: Write to Event Log
nodes.summary: Persists the alert event with timestamp, severity, source module, and details to the local event log for incident history.

nodes.name: Request Admin Privileges
nodes.summary: Requests root/admin elevation at startup since most security modules require privileged access to manipulate network interfaces, firewall rules, and packet capture.

nodes.name: Load Configuration
nodes.summary: Reads the user's configuration file to determine which modules to enable, VPN server settings, DNS resolver preferences, and UI options.

nodes.name: Initialize Modules
nodes.summary: Creates and configures instances of all enabled security modules (VPN, firewall, DNS, detection, anonymization, alerts) based on the loaded configuration.

nodes.name: Start System Tray
nodes.summary: Launches the system tray application with the dashboard showing current protection status, active threats, and trust score.

nodes.name: Graceful Shutdown
nodes.summary: Handles SIGTERM/SIGINT signals to cleanly shut down all modules in reverse dependency order, ensuring no network state is left modified.

nodes.name: Revert All Network Changes
nodes.summary: Restores the original MAC address, hostname, firewall rules, and routing table to their pre-app state, ensuring the network is left clean.

edges.description: Network Protection provides active tunnel/firewall state that Detection modules use to contextualize threats, and automatically activates VPN when Detection reports low trust scores or active attacks.
VPN tunnel provides the transport layer for DNS leak prevention. Network Protection and Anonymity must coordinate changes to the network stack (MAC, routing, DNS) to avoid conflicts.
Detection modules emit threat events (evil twin found, ARP spoof detected) that the Alert System renders as real-time notifications and writes to the event log.
Network Protection sends status events (VPN connected/disconnected, kill switch triggered, firewall applied) to the Alert System for user notification.
Anonymity modules report failures (MAC randomization failed, DNS leak attempt detected) to the Alert System for immediate user notification.
Core Engine orchestrates startup/shutdown of all Network Protection modules and propagates configuration changes (VPN server, firewall presets).
Core Engine starts/stops Anonymity modules and handles privilege escalation needed for MAC spoofing and packet capture.
Core Engine initializes Detection modules with libpcap access and provides system tray UI for displaying trust scores and detection status.
Core Engine manages the system tray that displays alerts and provides the event log viewer interface.
Detection domain feeds trust scores and threat intelligence back to Network Protection to trigger automatic VPN activation and firewall tightening.
